timeline_html:
Summary. Toa’s shedding began about 4 months ago and has been gradually worsening since then, which aligns with a period of sustained high stress over the past year. No new baby hairs are visible yet, suggesting her follicles are still in the active shedding phase.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Chronic stress (ongoing; started Jun 2025)

Timeline forecast. Chronic stress often drives ongoing shedding until the underlying pressure eases. Based on her reported onset and timing, the heaviest shedding likely peaked around 2026‑02 and may start to plateau over the next few months once stress levels improve. Calmer shedding and early regrowth are expected by 2026‑09 to 2026‑10. Dominant driver: Chronic stress is ongoing and overlaps fully with the onset of hair loss, making it the principal contributor.

Where you are now. You’re in the stabilization phase—shedding is still noticeable but beginning to slow once stress begins to lift. Keep supporting your stress recovery and scalp nourishment; new growth usually follows several months after balance returns. 🌱
